``` ::: ::: ::: {.panel-tabset .nav-pills} # Parole Eligibility ## How Parole Eligibility is Determined In general, parole eligibility occurs after serving 1/3 of the total sentence, and after a minimum of 9 months. Inmates serving 21 years or more, but not a life sentence, become eligible after serving a 7 year term. In addition, inmates convicted of certain serious crimes (e.g. aggravated assault, incest, drug trafficking) are not eligible for parole until they have served 7 years or 1/3 of the prison term, whichever comes first.
## In Prison Past Their Parole Eligibility Date Many people remain incarcerated past their parole eligibility date due to various reasons such as delays in the parole hearing process or unmet parole conditions. This section explores the breakdown of these individuals by type of prison admission and sentence length. - Show breakdown of types: 1) New court offenses/1-24.99 years 2) New court offenses/<1 & >= 25 years 3) Parole returns/1-24.99 years 4) Parole returns/<1 & >= 25 years - Show breakdown of people eligible for parole (currently eligible, missing parole eligibility data) - Demographics - Criminal Offense - Sentence Lengths
## Missing Data This section will have information about missing parole eligibility data for the state once new NCRP data is released. # Prison Population The size and composition of the prison population have significant implications for state resources and public safety. Sentence about change in year-end population from 2010 to 2020. Trend graph
## Demographics An examination of the demographic composition of Georgia's prison population, including age, gender, race, and other relevant factors.
## Criminal Offenses An overview of the types of criminal offenses that lead to incarceration, providing insight into the most common crimes and their prevalence.
## Sentence Lengths Analysis of the distribution of sentence lengths within the prison population. # Prison Releases Understanding trends in prison releases is crucial for assessing the effectiveness of parole and reentry programs. Sentence about change in releases from 2010 to 2020. Trend graph ## Prison Release Types
## Demographics A demographic analysis of individuals released from prison, highlighting trends and patterns in reentry.
## Timing of Release An exploration of the timing of prison releases, including how long individuals serve before being released.
## Time Served by Offense Type Detailed analysis of the average time served by individuals based on their offense type, providing insights into sentencing practices and their outcomes. # Disparities Examining disparities within the prison system is essential for addressing issues of equity and justice.
